reasoning: All queries pass correctness but are 10-17x slower than DuckDB. Two critical storage optimizations are missing from the baseline implementation: dictionary encoding for strings and sorted storage with zone maps. These are low-risk, high-impact changes that will close the performance gap significantly (expected to reach 1-3x slower than DuckDB).
focus_areas: Dictionary encoding for l_returnflag, l_linestatus, c_mktsegment
Sorted storage + zone maps on l_shipdate and o_orderdate
strategy_notes: Implement storage optimizations first (dictionary encoding + zone maps), then leverage dictionary encoding to enable direct-indexed aggregation for Q1. These three optimizations are complementary: dictionary encoding reduces string overhead and enables perfect hashing, zone maps enable block pruning for date filters. Together they target all three queries with foundational improvements. Save SIMD and advanced techniques for iteration 3 after establishing these core optimizations.
notes: Priority: Start with dictionary encoding (recommendation 0) as it's required for the direct-indexed aggregation (recommendation 2). Zone maps (recommendation 1) can be implemented in parallel. Ensure data is actually sorted during load before building zone maps. Expected iteration 2 results: Q1 ~200-250ms (10x improvement), Q3 ~400ms (5x improvement), Q6 ~40ms (6x improvement).
